blocks.body: Be used to / Get used to / Used to helps you separate being accustomed, becoming accustomed, and past habits.

{"level":"B1-B2","intro":"In this lesson you will separate three similar-looking forms: be used to, get used to, and used to. They look alike, but they talk about different things: being accustomed now, the process of adapting, and a past habit or state.","rules":[{"label":"BE USED TO","color":"emerald","text":"Be used to + noun / -ing = be accustomed to something now.","example":"I am used to working late."},{"label":"GET USED TO","color":"blue","text":"Get used to + noun / -ing = gradually become accustomed to something.","example":"She is getting used to her new job."},{"label":"USED TO","color":"amber","text":"Used to + base verb = a past habit or state that is no longer true now.","example":"We used to live near the sea."}]}

{"title":"1. Three different patterns","intro":"The form looks similar, but the grammar and meaning are different.","rows":[{"en":"be used to + noun / -ing","ua":"be accustomed to something; it feels normal now","note":"She is used to the noise. / She is used to working at night."},{"en":"get used to + noun / -ing","ua":"become accustomed little by little; go through a period of adaptation","note":"He is getting used to the weather."},{"en":"used to + base verb","ua":"have a past habit or past state that no longer exists now","note":"I used to play tennis every weekend."}],"warning":"⚠ After be used to and get used to, do not use the bare verb: you need a noun or a gerund. After used to, it is the opposite: you need the base verb."}

{"title":"2. How each form works","sections":[{"label":"Be used to = already accustomed","color":"emerald","description":"This pattern describes a state: something is already familiar and no longer difficult or surprising.","examples":[{"en":"I am used to the cold now.","ua":"I am used to the cold now."},{"en":"She is used to speaking in public.","ua":"She is used to speaking in public."},{"en":"We are used to long meetings.","ua":"We are used to long meetings."}]},{"label":"Get used to = become accustomed","color":"blue","description":"This pattern shows the process of adaptation, when something gradually becomes normal.","examples":[{"en":"It took me a month to get used to the schedule.","ua":"It took me a month to get used to the schedule."},{"en":"You will get used to driving on the left.","ua":"You will get used to driving on the left."},{"en":"She is getting used to living alone.","ua":"She is getting used to living alone."}],"note":"Here you can feel movement from unfamiliar to familiar."},{"label":"Used to = past habit or past state","color":"amber","description":"This pattern talks about a past habit or state that no longer exists now. On this page, the focus is on the form used to + base verb, not on the contrast with would.","examples":[{"en":"I used to drink a lot of coffee.","ua":"I used to drink a lot of coffee."},{"en":"They used to live in a small village.","ua":"They used to live in a small village."},{"en":"My dad used to walk to work.","ua":"My dad used to walk to work."}],"note":"After used to, use the base verb without -ing."}]}

{"title":"3. Common mistakes","items":[{"label":"Mistake 1","color":"rose","title":"Using a bare verb after be used to.","wrong":"I am used to work late.","right":"✅ I am used to working late.","hint":"After be used to, you need a noun or a gerund."},{"label":"Mistake 2","color":"amber","title":"Using a bare verb after get used to.","wrong":"She is getting used to live alone.","right":"✅ She is getting used to living alone.","hint":"Here too, you need the -ing form or a noun."},{"label":"Mistake 3","color":"sky","title":"Using a gerund after used to for a past habit.","wrong":"We used to living near the station.","right":"✅ We used to live near the station.","hint":"After used to in the meaning of a past habit, use the base verb."}]}

{"title":"4. Quick takeaway","items":["Be used to + noun / -ing = something is already familiar now.","Get used to + noun / -ing = a person or situation is still adapting.","Used to + base verb = a past habit or state that is no longer true now.","After be used to and get used to, think of to as part of the expression before a noun or gerund, not as the infinitive marker.","If you want to say “I regularly did this in the past”, check the pattern used to + base verb first."]}
